free_text: Resonances in 11C observed in the 4He(7Be,alpha)7Be
and 4He(7Be,p)10B reactions
The State University of New Jersey,
Piscataway,New Jersey
The technique of resonance scattering with a thick
target was used.It involves the passage of a beam
through a target material. As the beam slows down and
the center-of-mass energy coincides with that of a
resonance of the composite system then a resonant
component to the scattering process occurs. Inverse
kinematics was used and the resonant decay by
light particle emission was observed at zero degrees.
The 7Be was produced by first irradiating a natural
lithium target with a beam of 27-MeV protons and then
the 7Be (T1/2 = 53.2 days) was chemically separated
and installed into an ion source.
Statistical uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
Data presented in fig. 3 of the reference
sent by author (M.F.), LLN data
First experiment was performed
using cyclotron at Louvain-la-Neuve
The silicon array used for the measurements was
housed within the sample gas volume. The array was
arranged in a LEDA-type configuration composed of 8
YY1-type silicon strip detectors of thickness 500
mu-m. The plane of the array was placed 69 cm from
the entrance window and the detectors covered a range
of angles from 4.1. to 10.7. with respect to the
chamber window. Time of flight relative to cyclotron
frequency was measured.
He gas target with length of 69 cm at He pressure
of 110 mbar
Data presented in fig. 3 of the reference
sent by author (M.F.) ORNL data
Second set of measurements was
performed using a previously prepared 7Be sample
placed in the ion source of the HRIBF tandem
accelerator at ORNL
The Si E-dE telescope was capable of
distinguishing protons from heavier elements, i.e.
alpha particles. Detectors were calibrated using
241Am and 244Cm alpha sources.
He gas target with length of 38 cm at He pressure
of 920 mbar
Data presented in fig. 3 of the reference
